### examples/celery/src/task_app/views.py
```
def result(id: str) → dict[str, object]
def add() → dict[str, object]
def block() → dict[str, object]
def process() → dict[str, object]
GET /result/<id>  →  result()
POST /add  →  add()
POST /block  →  block()
POST /process  →  process()
```

### examples/tutorial/flaskr/auth.py
```
def login_required(view)  # View decorator that redirects anonymous users to the login p
def load_logged_in_user()  # If a user id is stored in the session, load the user object 
def register()  # Register a new user
def login()  # Log in a registered user by adding the user id to the sessio
def logout()  # Clear the current session, including the stored user id

### examples/tutorial/flaskr/blog.py
```
def index()  # Show all the posts, most recent first
def get_post(id, check_author)  # Get a post and its author by id
def create()  # Create a new post for the current user
def update(id)  # Update a post if the current user is the author
def delete(id)  # Delete a post
```

class AuthActions
  def __init__(client)
  def login(username, password)
  def logout()
def app()  # Create and configure a new app instance for each test
def client(app)  # A test client for the app
def runner(app)  # A test runner for the app's Click commands
def auth(client)
```
